# nestegg.money

> A private, zero-knowledge net worth and salary tracker. Figures are encrypted in the browser with a key derived from a random account number, so the server only ever stores ciphertext it cannot read.

nestegg also exposes its deterministic finance math as stateless calculators that any client, including AI agents, can call. The calculators are pure functions of their inputs. They never read user data, never fetch live prices or exchange rates, and carry no secrets. Any currency conversion takes the rate as an explicit input. They return numbers and schedules, never financial advice.

## Calculators

- [Calculator reference](https://nestegg.money/docs/calculators.md): description, inputs, and outputs for every calculator (94 in total). Coverage spans loans & mortgages (amortization with multi-rate steps, payoff, APR, balloon, biweekly, points, LTV/DTI, affordability), time value of money (present/future value, annuities, perpetuities, NPV/IRR, XNPV/XIRR, MIRR, payback), investing & risk (ROI, CAGR, Sharpe, drawdown, return stats, fee drag, dollar-cost averaging), bonds (price, yield to maturity, duration, convexity), options (Black-Scholes, greeks, parity), retirement (FIRE/coast/barista, portfolio longevity, contributions), real estate (cap rate, cash-on-cash, NOI, DSCR), business (WACC, break-even, margins, ratios, depreciation methods), tax (progressive brackets, after-tax & tax-equivalent yield, German gross-to-net, VAT), and everyday money (net worth, budgeting, tips, discounts, unit price, hourly/salary). The calculators bake in no dated tax tables or rates: where current statutory numbers are needed (for example German payroll), the caller passes them in. The doc is generated from the registry, so it always matches the live tools.

## API

- JSON endpoints: `POST https://nestegg.money/api/calc/<name>` with a JSON body, JSON out. `GET https://nestegg.money/api/calc` lists the calculators. CORS-open, no auth, no storage.
- MCP server (Streamable HTTP): `https://nestegg.money/mcp` exposes the same calculators as MCP tools (initialize, tools/list, tools/call) with typed outputSchema and read-only annotations. It also serves `resources/*` (the calculator docs) and `prompts/*` (mortgage-plan, fire-check, brutto-netto). Large results are compact by default: `amortization` returns a per-year summary unless `detail: "monthly"` is requested (paginated with offset/limit).

## App

- [Live app](https://nestegg.money): the tracker. A no-account demo loads sample data at https://nestegg.money/dashboard#demo.
- [Source](https://github.com/VladimirWrites/nestegg.money): open source (Apache-2.0).

## Notes

- Zero-knowledge: the server cannot read user financial data. This work does not touch the encryption, sync, or storage layers.
- The calculators store nothing, require no authentication, and do not use live market data. Money is rounded half-up to two decimals.
